description: Lapras lead, Shadow Kingdra safe swap, and Abomasnow in the back for Retro Cup. Built on the idea of pairing a dragon with water Pokémon, leaning on neutral coverage and ice damage from Abomasnow as a 'corebreaker'.
performanceNotes: Creator considers Abomasnow a great Pokémon/corebreaker for this meta (handles Blastoise, Furret, etc.)
Shadow is seen as a must on Kingdra since the Swift/Dragon Breath output more damage
Team is double weak to fighting and double weak to rock, struggling vs Annihilape and Medicham

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: ABOMASNOW the COREBREAKER of RETRO CUP | Retro Cup Team | Pokemon GO Battle League

description: Three highly-ranked, very bulky Pokémon that gain extra play with Dark/Steel/Fairy gone. Flexible team where any of the three can be the sweeper depending on the matchup.
performanceNotes: Oranguru and Lickilicky provide safe, flexible play while Araquanid leans on Bug Bite fast-move damage
Confusion no longer resisted, giving Oranguru a boost; team lets you pick your sweeper per matchup

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: BEST RETRO CUP TEAMS | TOP RANKED POKÉMON | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Regidrago lead paired with a personal Aurorus to cover the Ice weakness, backed by a spammy Golisopod safe swap. Aurorus as a Rock/Ice resists Ice and hits back super effectively with Rock Wrecker.
performanceNotes: Aurorus covers Regidrago's Ice weakness while resisting Ice itself
Golisopod (half-Bug) gains freedom with fewer Bug resists in the meta and offers great neutral coverage

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: BEST RETRO CUP TEAMS | TOP RANKED POKÉMON | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Ledian lead running Bug Bite (over the new Roll Out) into the psychic-heavy Retro Cup, backed by Primeape and Shadow Aurorus. Ledian absorbs energy and applies fast bug pressure to set up Aurorus or Primeape sweeps.
performanceNotes: Bug Bite Ledian obliterates Furret and Ounces/Oranguru-type normals despite slower pacing
Primeape wins charge-stack priority over Annihilape and stores Rage Fist for super-effective KOs
Shadow Aurorus cleans up and swept full teams once set up; team struggles into Annihilape/low-kick variants

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: FURRET'S NOT SAFE! Dynamic Punch Ledian feasts in the Retro Cup

description: Lapras lead with Goodra dragon to cover the Ice weakness, plus Grumpig as a safe swap. Lapras handles Ice Pokémon while the bulky dragon Goodra pairs freely with everything.
performanceNotes: Lapras as Water/Ice does well vs Ice while the dragon covers its own Ice weakness
Grumpig spams Dynamic Punch / Shadow Ball with Psywave STAB, single weak to Bug
